title: The AI Power Infrastructure Trade Has Never Been Stronger, But One Space Race Could Change That
author: Opeyemi Babalola
description: Hut 8 and Fluence Energy have secured major contracts positioning them as critical suppliers for AI infrastructure. Hut 8 signed a $9.8 billion 15-year lease at its Beacon Point campus, while Fluence qualified as a pre-qualified supplier to major hyperscalers for energy storage. However, emerging orbital data center projects from Google and SpaceX pose a long-term structural risk to ground-based power infrastructure investments, though economics remain unfavorable before 2030.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Secured a $9.8 billion 15-year triple-net take-or-pay lease with high-investment-grade tenant, stock jumped 30%, and locked in $655 million average annual NOI. Strong near-term catalysts with Q1 2027 energization and Phase 2 leasing opportunities.

Qualified as pre-qualified global supplier to at least two major hyperscalers, won competitive procurement processes, and has $5.6 billion contracted backlog. Q3 2026 first hyperscaler order represents key catalyst converting MSAs to revenue.

Pursuing Project Suncatcher orbital data center prototype launching in 2027, which represents a potential long-term structural risk to ground-based infrastructure plays, but economics remain unfavorable and uncertain.

Pursuing Project Suncatcher orbital data center prototype launching in 2027, which represents a potential long-term structural risk to ground-based infrastructure plays, but economics remain unfavorable and uncertain.

Benefits as foundational layer of AI infrastructure stack; Beacon Point facility uses Nvidia DSX reference architecture, and CEO Jensen Huang's framework validates the importance of power infrastructure to AI deployment.
Execution partner for Beacon Point campus with interconnection agreement for 1,000 MW utility capacity, positioning it to benefit from AI infrastructure buildout.
Execution partner for Beacon Point campus infrastructure, positioned to benefit from AI data center buildout.
Execution partner for Beacon Point campus development, benefiting from AI infrastructure construction and engineering services.

title: Jacobs Increases Quarterly Dividend
author: Prnewswire
description: Jacobs Solutions Inc. announced a 12.5% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.36 per share, up from the previous $0.32 per share. The dividend will be paid on March 20, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 20, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company announced a 12.5% increase in quarterly dividend payments, demonstrating confidence in financial performance and commitment to returning capital to shareholders. Dividend increases are typically viewed as a positive signal of company health and profitability.

title: Jacobs Bags UK Public Sector Advisory Contract Through 2027
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Jacobs Solutions has secured a contract with the UK Crown Commercial Service's Management Consultancy Framework to provide infrastructure advisory services through 2027, leveraging a strategic partnership with PA Consulting.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company won a significant multi-year public sector contract, expanding its advisory services and demonstrating market strength

title: Jacobs (J) Q3 EPS Jumps 25% Beats Views
author: Jesterai
description: Jacobs Solutions reported Q3 FY2025 earnings with non-GAAP EPS of $1.62, beating analyst estimates by 5.9%. Revenue was $3.03 billion, slightly below forecasts, but the company showed strong performance in digital consulting, life sciences, and infrastructure sectors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong EPS growth of 24.6%, record backlog of $22.7 billion, raised full-year guidance, and strategic focus on high-growth sectors like digital transformation and sustainability

title: Energy Consulting Market worth $26.36 billion by 2032 - Exclusive Report by Coherent Market Insights
author: Coherent Market Insights
description: The global energy consulting market is expected to grow from USD 18.11 Bn in 2025 to USD 26.36 Bn by 2032, driven by the increasing focus on renewable energy and sustainability. Key factors include rising energy efficiency demands, the transition to clean energy, and the adoption of AI-powered tools by consulting firms.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Accenture acquired Boslan, a prominent engineering and consulting firm, to bolster its offerings in net-zero infrastructure projects, indicating the company's focus on the growing renewable energy and sustainability consulting market.
Jacobs Engineering Group is mentioned as a key player in the energy consulting market, but the article does not offer any updates or insights about the company's involvement or growth in this sector.
